Ive heard really conflicting stuff about Horla E2. Ive heard some say she really wants to have this and its a good spot to stop at.

Ive heard others say E2 straightup makes her worse. Can anyone clarify?

My own Horla is E1 right now. Shes one of the 4 I choose for the Moonless guide banner.

Right now I want to keep pulling because the other 3 awakeners are all still good pulls for me
But Im not sure where Horla E2 stands.
Its situational
You can also get the shard but not enlighten
But basically she retains her cards
Which if you need them next turn great
If not
Theyre just clogging up ur hand space which couldve been used to draw other cards
I guess its bad if you only stick with one emotion?
Not necessarily
For example gelot teams try to stay in anger 100% of the time bc the others dont really help much
Other than maybe happiness pre boss fight to maximize aliemus
Afain
Again
Very situational
Play around
This is my team currently. The ones I choose for the moonless guide are Tawil, GHelot, Horla and Thais
Should I keep going?
People typically say to stop when u get ur first milestone
In my humble opinion
I like gambling
Not that situational.
You just hold her cards, it's free deck fixing.
Unless you have a DPS that needs to stock up cards in your hand over time, it's probably fine.
And Happiness makes you retain all the cards *anyways* so who cares?
As a gelot main
I care
I need my hand space to copy more flails
e2 is good
See, *that's* an excellent reason not to get Horla E2.
And the only card i wouldnt play immediately is defense
GHelot is a good example of someone who's bad with Horla E2.
not true
weakness on demand is good
*could be
Same with Kath.
But otherwise, E2 is free deck fixing.
And you can hold cards so you can guarantee the right emotion next turn.
how is kath bad
u swap stances from sad to anger
during hyperflare turn
e2 makes that more consistent
*could be*
I'm not saying it *is*.
For example I think it'd do fine in my Kath team.

she doesnt provide crit, vuln weakness, or aligen buffs outside of that yes

So what is your current comp?
The preferred comp is Salv/Thais/Horla/Arachne, with other substitutes being Pickman, Winkle, and Xu.
Also, what are your Wheels/Covenants? Have you got a screenshot of the current composition?
jad the goat
ik the levels are low but I tried this on the arcade where they increase the levels
I have horla and arachne but I mainly want to use arachne with liz
Arcade is painful because Salvador is bugged there.
oh what
So without careful play or heavy investment his healing is quartered.
oh lol
but still at most I did 30k with his 2 cost
So in other stages, you want sources of healing bonus - try Data is Flesh instead of Analysis of Death on him.
And generally lean towards things which can provide a source of healing bonuses like Horla, Singularity Ultra, things like that.
24's generally pretty low value because her sources of Furnace are low compared to how much Salvador generates himself.

Additionally, most of the time when playing the comp, you want to keep a copy of 2 cost in hand as a basis for Thais/Lone Seed duping, and when playing 2 cosst, usually use HP -> Furnace mode, then Furnace -> Shield mode to generate large shields.
OE when possible to increase cap and when opponents are gonna do a big hit for free furnace.
won't he have too low of a crit rate that way?
You can compensate that with Covenants.
that makes sense but when do you use the damage mode?

You use it when you:

Are at full health or close to it.
Have enough shield to tank a hit.
Have capped furnace.
Damage mode is the least used but it's about knowing when you can use it.
Also if you want to test out a broken Salvador add my UID, I have a maxed out one you can borrow.
doesn't he do like no damage in general then?
kinda beats the purpose of me having an oe salv then lmao
No because 2 cost gains damage equal to you having the total furnace gained in the battle.
So as you convert HP into Furnace you steadily increase the damage dealt by your 2 costs every time.
Yeah reasonable, was offering mostly as a build showcase lmao.
But yeah in general your Damage option is largely for a Burst Damage option.
oh I see
Whereas most of the time you gain damage over time by ramping furnace and blocking hits.
ah yeah I can do that

This is also why Healing Bonus is so strong - if you have say, 50% healing bonus, then it becomes:

10% current HP loss
15% current HP into the furnace.
Which means after spending some HP to make furnace for a shield, you can spend more to heal and go positive on HP.
Combine that with OE on the big hits to gain a bunch of furnace for free and you essentially loop HP forever.
so you're just building damage over a bunch of turns
Yep.
Newbie here! How do you play/survive with Divine Aqueor team? Normal Aqueor team you could just switch to Stagnant Sea whenever you want. Kinda struggling with the 3 turn cd of Divine.
Essentially your healing/shield gain is acting as passive "Strength" Generation.

Run an alternative shielding source so that you can alternate between them
